steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank is preceded by a participial phrase—”Fortifying soil in arid ecosystems against erosion,”—which must attach to the subject of the clause that follows.

steps.title: Apply the rule: participial phrases must modify appropriate subjects
steps.content:
A participial phrase modifies the noun that immediately follows it, and that noun must logically be capable of performing the action.

Choice C provides “these crusts” as the subject, and these crusts logically fortify soil against erosion.

The other three provide subjects that cannot logically fortify soil.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
